title: DOJ Now Citing Fake AI-Generated Cases To Keep ICE Detainees Locked Up
excerpt: You’d think that watching multiple lawyers get caught red-handed using AI to file briefs full of made-up citations would cause everyone in the legal profession — especially prosecutors — to d…
content_html:
from the this-must-not-happen-in-a-free-society dept

You’d think that watching multiple lawyers get caught red-handed using AI to file briefs full of made-up citations would cause everyone in the legal profession — especially prosecutors — to double-check their own. You would, of course, be wrong. And the latest offender is not just some random lawyer. It’s the DOJ itself, which cited a nonexistent Sixth Circuit case to argue that an ICE detainee shouldn’t be able to challenge a stay that prevented him from posting a bond that had already been granted, thereby leaving him in detention.

This story hits on two different threads we’ve been covering over the last few years separately. Having them collide somehow makes both worse.

We’ve covered plenty of cases where lawyers for one party (or both, or sometimes judges) are misusing AI to do their writing for them, generating fictitious cases in support of whatever argument they’re seeking to make. This is troubling on many levels, because one of the things any lawyer is supposed to do before submitting anything to a court is check the citations. Historically that has been to make sure the cases cited haven’t been overruled. In these cases, not only is that not happening, they’re literally putting in cases that don’t exist, citing precedents that are completely fictitious.

Our other line of stories touched on here is how ICE and the DOJ have been stomping all over detainees’ basic constitutional rights.

This is one of many cases in which lawyers for a detainee have filed a habeas petition — and it’s a clean example of those two threads colliding in practice. I’ll let Judge Hala Y. Jarbou summarize the basics of what happened:

Petitioner, a United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement detainee, initiated this action by filing a petition for a writ of habeas corpus pursuant to 28 U.S.C. § 2241. (Pet., ECF No. 1.) An immigration judge had granted Petitioner a bond of $35,000, but the bond order was stayed pending appeal to the Board of Immigration Appeals pursuant to 8 C.F.R. § 1003.19(i) (2025). Petitioner argued that the 90-day automatic stay provision in § 1003.19(i) violates the Fifth Amendment’s Due Process Clause, and sought an order requiring the Government to allow him to post bond. While this lawsuit was pending, the automatic stay of Petitioner’s bond order expired. The Government now represents that the bond order is back in effect and Petitioner will be released if he posts the $35,000 bond. (Status Report, ECF No. 10.) Accordingly, the Court finds that the habeas petition is moot and dismisses it without prejudice.

Already frustrating enough that the 90-day “automatic stay” that the detainee was challenging ended before the actual case could be decided, making the whole thing moot.

But… there’s something else the judge had on her mind. The DOJ appeared to have a totally fabricated citation in an earlier filing:

There is one additional issue in this case that the Court must address. In the Government’s response to the Court’s initial order to show cause, it stated the following:

More recently, the Sixth Circuit has reiterated that § 1226(e) bars challenges that “ask the court to reweigh the evidence underlying a bond decision or second-guess the Immigration Judge’s discretionary judgment.” See Taylor v. Hott, 724 F. App’x 387, 392 (6th Cir. 2018) (district court lacked jurisdiction to review IJ’s bond denial where petitioner challenged flight-risk determination) . . . .

(Gov’t’s Resp. 9, ECF No. 5.) The cited case, Taylor v. Hott, is not located at the identified page of the Federal Appendix. Indeed, page 387 is contained within a different opinion—Atkins v. CGI Techs. & Sols., Inc., 724 F. App’x 383 (6th Cir. 2018)—which is about commercial arbitration, not immigration bond determinations. In its research, the Court was unable to identify a Sixth Circuit case with the caption Taylor v. Hott, or any federal case containing the quoted language. Thus, it seems this citation was likely produced by generative artificial intelligence (“AI”).

It is already bad enough when you have a fabricated citation in a civil case between two private parties. But here we’re literally talking about a case involving someone’s freedom. And the government is filing AI-generated fake cases?!?

We should be livid. But the judge lets them off with a little slap on the wrist and a “please don’t do this again”:

It should be obvious that any attorney who uses AI must scrupulously review its work product to ensure that the cited cases exist and that the citations accurately and fairly represent the underlying case law. The duty of candor towards this tribunal demands no less.

Although the Court will not presently impose sanctions for this conduct, it goes without saying that the Government must ensure its future filings with this Court do not include nonexistent case law

And, yes, judges tend to be fairly restrained in issuing sanctions, often giving misbehaving lawyers many more chances than they probably deserve before rushing to punishment. But, again, this was someone’s freedom on the line. And the DOJ literally directly — whether intentionally or not — misled the court with a precedent that doesn’t exist. That should never happen.

We all know the DOJ is having trouble staffing lawyers. Over 10,000 lawyers have left the federal government since Donald Trump came back into office. They’re offering large bonuses for jobs that lawyers used to deliberately take massive pay cuts to get on their resume. Pretty much everyone agrees it’s a staffing crisis, to the point that earlier this year an apparently exhausted Assistant US Attorney, Julie Le, asked an angry judge to find her in contempt just so she could get some sleep:

Attorney Julie Le was representing the government at a hearing over ICE’s failure to follow court orders and immediately release people that it had wrongfully detained. When Judge Jerry Blackwell asked why the agency is not complying, Le said that the government was “overwhelmed” by the legal challenges to Operation Metro Surge in Minnesota, and that trying to get ICE to comply with court orders has required nonstop work for an office depleted by resignations. 

“I wish you would just hold me in contempt of court so I can get 24 hours of sleep,” Le said. “The system sucks, this job sucks, I am trying with every breath I have to get you what I need.” 

Given that kind of work environment, is it really any surprise that the few remaining DOJ lawyers would turn to hallucinating AI tools to “generate” their legal filings?

But if you’re starting to feel any ounce of sympathy for the DOJ here, let’s be clear: fuck that. If the DOJ can’t do their fucking job they shouldn’t be throwing people into jails, detainment centers, concentration camps, or anything of that nature. If they want to go around fighting habeas petitions, maybe don’t lock up so many people without any ounce of due process. And if they want to keep people detained then hire enough lawyers to handle the government’s case load.

And if that’s too difficult because good lawyers have no interest in working for a fascist regime that is stomping all over basic fundamental freedoms in every direction, well, maybe work on that rather than locking innocent people up with no due process. Donald Trump’s administration put this DOJ in this position and there’s simply zero excuse for judges letting the DOJ get away with this sort of absolute bullshit.

Judges should be issuing sanctions left and right. They should be reporting lawyers to ethics committees and the relevant bar associations. They should be demanding that the government actually obey the fucking law, and not deny anyone their rights.

Want to stop the DOJ from locking people up with fictitious cases? Start issuing actual consequences to those lawyers and anyone else at the DOJ who had anything to do with this.

Filed Under: ai, ai hallucinations, doj, hala jarbou, ice detentions, izzeddin daghra, julie le

sentences.zh: 来自“这绝不能发生在自由社会”部门

blocks.html_en: You’d think that watching multiple lawyers get caught red-handed using AI to file briefs full of made-up citations would cause everyone in the legal profession — especially prosecutors — to double-check their own. You would, of course, be wrong. And the latest offender is not just some random lawyer. sentences.en: You’d think that watching multiple lawyers get caught red-handed using AI to file briefs full of made-up citations would cause everyone in the legal profession — especially prosecutors — to double-check their own.
sentences.zh: 你可能会认为，看到多名律师使用人工智能提交充满捏造引文的摘要而被当场抓获，会导致法律界的每个人——尤其是检察官——仔细检查自己的陈述。

sentences.en: You would, of course, be wrong.
sentences.zh: 当然，你错了。

sentences.en: And the latest offender is not just some random lawyer.
sentences.zh: 最新的犯罪者不仅仅是某个随意的律师。

sentences.en: It’s the DOJ itself, which cited a nonexistent Sixth Circuit case to argue that an ICE detainee shouldn’t be able to challenge a stay that prevented him from posting a bond that had already been granted, thereby leaving him in detention.
sentences.zh: 正是司法部本身引用了一个不存在的第六巡回法院案件，认为 ICE 被拘留者不应能够对阻止他缴纳已获准保释金的暂缓提出质疑，从而导致他被拘留。

sentences.en: This story hits on two different threads we’ve been covering over the last few years separately.
sentences.zh: 这个故事涉及我们过去几年分别报道的两个不同的主题。

sentences.en: Having them collide somehow makes both worse.
sentences.zh: 让它们发生碰撞会使两者变得更糟。

This is troubling on many levels, because one of the things any lawyer is supposed to do before submitting anything to a court is check the citations. Historically that has been to make sure the cases cited haven’t been overruled. In these cases, not only is that not happening, they’re literally putting in cases that don’t exist, citing precedents that are completely fictitious.

sentences.zh: 我们已经报道过很多案件，其中一方（或双方，有时是法官）的律师滥用人工智能为他们写作，生成虚构的案件来支持他们想要提出的任何论点。

sentences.en: This is troubling on many levels, because one of the things any lawyer is supposed to do before submitting anything to a court is check the citations.
sentences.zh: 这在很多层面上都令人不安，因为任何律师在向法院提交任何内容之前应该做的事情之一就是检查引文。

sentences.en: Historically that has been to make sure the cases cited haven’t been overruled.
sentences.zh: 从历史上看，这是为了确保所引用的案例没有被推翻。

sentences.en: In these cases, not only is that not happening, they’re literally putting in cases that don’t exist, citing precedents that are completely fictitious.
sentences.zh: 在这些情况下，这种情况不仅没有发生，而且他们实际上是在引用不存在的案例，引用完全虚构的先例。

sentences.en: Our other line of stories touched on here is how ICE and the DOJ have been stomping all over detainees’ basic constitutional rights.
sentences.zh: 我们在此涉及的其他故事是 ICE 和司法部如何践踏被拘留者的基本宪法权利。

sentences.en: This is one of many cases in which lawyers for a detainee have filed a habeas petition — and it’s a clean example of those two threads colliding in practice.
sentences.zh: 这是被拘留者律师提交人身保护令的众多案件之一，也是这两条线索在实践中发生冲突的一个明显例子。

sentences.en: I’ll let Judge Hala Y.
sentences.zh: 我会让哈拉·Y 法官来。

sentences.en: Jarbou summarize the basics of what happened:
sentences.zh: Jarbou 总结了所发生事件的基本情况：

blocks.html_en: Petitioner, a United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement detainee, initiated this action by filing a petition for a writ of habeas corpus pursuant to 28 U.S.C. § 2241. (Pet., ECF No. 1.) An immigration judge had granted Petitioner a bond of $35,000, but the bond order was stayed pending appeal to the Board of Immigration Appeals pursuant to 8 C.F.R. § 1003.19(i) (2025). Petitioner argued that the 90-day automatic stay provision in § 1003.19(i) violates the Fifth Amendment’s Due Process Clause, and sought an order requiring the Government to allow him to post bond. While this lawsuit was pending, the automatic stay of Petitioner’s bond order expired. The Government now represents that the bond order is back in effect and Petitioner will be released if he posts the $35,000 bond. (Status Report, ECF No. 10.) Accordingly, the Court finds that the habeas petition is moot and dismisses it without prejudice.

sentences.en: Petitioner, a United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement detainee, initiated this action by filing a petition for a writ of habeas corpus pursuant to 28 U.S.C.
sentences.zh: 申请人是一名被美国移民和海关执法局拘留的人，根据《美国法典》第 28 条的规定，通过提交人身保护令请愿书发起了这一诉讼。

sentences.en: (Pet., ECF No. 1.)
sentences.zh: （宠物，ECF No.1。）

sentences.en: An immigration judge had granted Petitioner a bond of $35,000, but the bond order was stayed pending appeal to the Board of Immigration Appeals pursuant to 8 C.F.R.
sentences.zh: 移民法官已向申请人提供 35,000 美元的保释金，但保释令被暂缓执行，等待根据 8 C.F.R. 向移民上诉委员会提出上诉。

sentences.en: Petitioner argued that the 90-day automatic stay provision in § 1003.19(i) violates the Fifth Amendment’s Due Process Clause, and sought an order requiring the Government to allow him to post bond.
sentences.zh: 申请人辩称，第 1003.19(i) 条中的 90 天自动中止条款违反了第五修正案的正当程序条款，并寻求一项命令，要求政府允许他交纳保释金。

sentences.en: While this lawsuit was pending, the automatic stay of Petitioner’s bond order expired.
sentences.zh: 在该诉讼悬而未决期间，申请人的保证金令自动中止到期。

sentences.en: The Government now represents that the bond order is back in effect and Petitioner will be released if he posts the $35,000 bond.
sentences.zh: 政府现在表示，保证金令重新生效，如果申请人缴纳 35,000 美元的保证金，他将被释放。

sentences.en: (Status Report, ECF No. 10.)
sentences.zh: （状态报告，ECF 第 10 号。）

sentences.en: Accordingly, the Court finds that the habeas petition is moot and dismisses it without prejudice.
sentences.zh: 因此，法院认为人身保护令申请没有实际意义，并在不造成偏见的情况下予以驳回。

sentences.zh: 本案还有一个法院必须解决的问题。

sentences.en: In the Government’s response to the Court’s initial order to show cause, it stated the following: More recently, the Sixth Circuit has reiterated that § 1226(e) bars challenges that “ask the court to reweigh the evidence underlying a bond decision or second-guess the Immigration Judge’s discretionary judgment.”
sentences.zh: 政府在对法院最初的说明理由命令的回应中指出：最近，第六巡回法院重申，第 1226(e) 条禁止提出质疑“要求法院重新权衡保释决定所依据的证据或事后怀疑移民法官的自由裁量判决”。

sentences.zh: 参见泰勒诉。

blocks.html_en: There is one additional issue in this case that the Court must address. In the Government’s response to the Court’s initial order to show cause, it stated the following:

sentences.en: There is one additional issue in this case that the Court must address.
sentences.zh: 本案还有一个法院必须解决的问题。

sentences.en: In the Government’s response to the Court’s initial order to show cause, it stated the following:
sentences.zh: 政府在对法院最初的出示理由命令的回应中声明如下：

blocks.html_en: More recently, the Sixth Circuit has reiterated that § 1226(e) bars challenges that “ask the court to reweigh the evidence underlying a bond decision or second-guess the Immigration Judge’s discretionary judgment.” See Taylor v. Hott, 724 F. App’x 387, 392 (6th Cir. 2018) (district court lacked jurisdiction to review IJ’s bond denial where petitioner challenged flight-risk determination) . . . .

sentences.en: More recently, the Sixth Circuit has reiterated that § 1226(e) bars challenges that “ask the court to reweigh the evidence underlying a bond decision or second-guess the Immigration Judge’s discretionary judgment.”
sentences.zh: 最近，第六巡回法院重申，第 1226(e) 条禁止质疑“要求法院重新权衡保释决定所依据的证据或事后怀疑移民法官的自由裁量判决”。

sentences.en: See Taylor v.
sentences.zh: 参见泰勒诉。

App’x 387, 392 (6th Cir.
2018) (district court lacked jurisdiction to review IJ’s bond denial where petitioner challenged flight-risk determination) . . . .
sentences.zh: 2018）（地方法院没有管辖权来审查 IJ 在申请人对潜逃风险判定提出质疑的情况下拒绝保释的情况）。 。 。 。

Hott, 724 F. App’x 387, 392 (6th Cir. 2018) (district court lacked jurisdiction to review IJ’s bond denial where petitioner challenged flight-risk determination) . . . .

blocks.html_en: (Gov’t’s Resp. 9, ECF No. 5.) The cited case, Taylor v. Hott, is not located at the identified page of the Federal Appendix. Indeed, page 387 is contained within a different opinion—Atkins v. CGI Techs. & Sols., Inc., 724 F. App’x 383 (6th Cir. 2018)—which is about commercial arbitration, not immigration bond determinations. In its research, the Court was unable to identify a Sixth Circuit case with the caption Taylor v. Hott, or any federal case containing the quoted language. Thus, it seems this citation was likely produced by generative artificial intelligence (“AI”).

sentences.zh: （政府的答复。

sentences.en: 2018)—which is about commercial arbitration, not immigration bond determinations.
sentences.zh: 2018）——这是关于商业仲裁，而不是移民债券的决定。

sentences.zh: 在其研究中，法院无法识别标题为泰勒诉泰勒案的第六巡回法院案件。

sentences.en: Hott, or any federal case containing the quoted language.
sentences.zh: 霍特，或任何包含引用语言的联邦案件。

sentences.en: Thus, it seems this citation was likely produced by generative artificial intelligence (“AI”).
sentences.zh: 因此，这条引文似乎很可能是由生成人工智能（“AI”）产生的。

blocks.html_en: It is already bad enough when you have a fabricated citation in a civil case between two private parties. But here we’re literally talking about a case involving someone’s freedom. And the government is filing AI-generated fake cases?!?

sentences.en: It is already bad enough when you have a fabricated citation in a civil case between two private parties.
sentences.zh: 当两个私人当事人之间的民事案件中出现捏造的传票时，情况就已经够糟糕的了。

sentences.en: But here we’re literally talking about a case involving someone’s freedom.
sentences.zh: 但这里我们实际上谈论的是涉及某人自由的案件。

sentences.en: And the government is filing AI-generated fake cases?!?
sentences.zh: 政府正在立案人工智能生成的假案件？！？

blocks.html_en: We should be livid. But the judge lets them off with a little slap on the wrist and a “please don’t do this again”:

sentences.en: We should be livid.
sentences.zh: 我们应该感到愤怒。

sentences.en: But the judge lets them off with a little slap on the wrist and a “please don’t do this again”:
sentences.zh: 但法官轻轻地打了他们一巴掌，并说：“请不要再这样做了”：

blocks.html_en:
It should be obvious that any attorney who uses AI must scrupulously review its work product to ensure that the cited cases exist and that the citations accurately and fairly represent the underlying case law. The duty of candor towards this tribunal demands no less.

Although the Court will not presently impose sanctions for this conduct, it goes without saying that the Government must ensure its future filings with this Court do not include nonexistent case law

sentences.en: It should be obvious that any attorney who uses AI must scrupulously review its work product to ensure that the cited cases exist and that the citations accurately and fairly represent the underlying case law.
sentences.zh: 显然，任何使用人工智能的律师都必须仔细审查其工作产品，以确保引用的案例存在，并且引用准确、公平地代表了基本的判例法。

sentences.en: The duty of candor towards this tribunal demands no less.
sentences.zh: 对本法庭的坦诚义务要求同样如此。

sentences.en: Although the Court will not presently impose sanctions for this conduct, it goes without saying that the Government must ensure its future filings with this Court do not include nonexistent case law
sentences.zh: 尽管法院目前不会对这种行为实施制裁，但毫无疑问，政府必须确保未来向法院提交的文件不包括不存在的判例法

blocks.html_en: It should be obvious that any attorney who uses AI must scrupulously review its work product to ensure that the cited cases exist and that the citations accurately and fairly represent the underlying case law. The duty of candor towards this tribunal demands no less.

sentences.en: It should be obvious that any attorney who uses AI must scrupulously review its work product to ensure that the cited cases exist and that the citations accurately and fairly represent the underlying case law.
sentences.zh: 显然，任何使用人工智能的律师都必须仔细审查其工作产品，以确保引用的案例存在，并且引用准确、公平地代表了基本的判例法。

sentences.en: The duty of candor towards this tribunal demands no less.
sentences.zh: 对本法庭的坦诚义务要求同样如此。

blocks.html_en: Although the Court will not presently impose sanctions for this conduct, it goes without saying that the Government must ensure its future filings with this Court do not include nonexistent case law

sentences.en: Although the Court will not presently impose sanctions for this conduct, it goes without saying that the Government must ensure its future filings with this Court do not include nonexistent case law
sentences.zh: 尽管法院目前不会对这种行为实施制裁，但毫无疑问，政府必须确保未来向法院提交的文件不包括不存在的判例法

blocks.html_en: And, yes, judges tend to be fairly restrained in issuing sanctions, often giving misbehaving lawyers many more chances than they probably deserve before rushing to punishment. But, again, this was someone’s freedom on the line. And the DOJ literally directly — whether intentionally or not — misled the court with a precedent that doesn’t exist. That should never happen.

sentences.en: And, yes, judges tend to be fairly restrained in issuing sanctions, often giving misbehaving lawyers many more chances than they probably deserve before rushing to punishment.
sentences.zh: 而且，是的，法官在发布制裁时往往相当克制，在急于进行惩罚之前，常常给行为不端的律师更多的机会，而他们可能不应该得到这样的机会。

sentences.en: But, again, this was someone’s freedom on the line.
sentences.zh: 但话又说回来，这事关某人的自由。

sentences.en: And the DOJ literally directly — whether intentionally or not — misled the court with a precedent that doesn’t exist.
sentences.zh: 司法部确实直接——无论是有意还是无意——以不存在的先例误导了法院。

sentences.en: That should never happen.
sentences.zh: 那绝对不应该发生。

blocks.html_en: We all know the DOJ is having trouble staffing lawyers. Over 10,000 lawyers have left the federal government since Donald Trump came back into office. They’re offering large bonuses for jobs that lawyers used to deliberately take massive pay cuts to get on their resume. Pretty much everyone agrees it’s a staffing crisis, to the point that earlier this year an apparently exhausted Assistant US Attorney, Julie Le, asked an angry judge to find her in contempt just so she could get some sleep:

sentences.en: We all know the DOJ is having trouble staffing lawyers.
sentences.zh: 我们都知道司法部在聘请律师方面遇到了麻烦。

sentences.en: Over 10,000 lawyers have left the federal government since Donald Trump came back into office.
sentences.zh: 自唐纳德·特朗普重新上任以来，已有超过 10,000 名律师离开联邦政府。

sentences.en: They’re offering large bonuses for jobs that lawyers used to deliberately take massive pay cuts to get on their resume.
sentences.zh: 他们为那些律师过去故意大幅减薪以写在简历上的工作提供巨额奖金。

sentences.en: Pretty much everyone agrees it’s a staffing crisis, to the point that earlier this year an apparently exhausted Assistant US Attorney, Julie Le, asked an angry judge to find her in contempt just so she could get some sleep:
sentences.zh: 几乎每个人都认为这是一场人员配置危机，以至于今年早些时候，一位显然疲惫不堪的美国助理检察官朱莉·勒 (Julie Le) 要求一位愤怒的法官认定她藐视法庭，这样她就可以睡一会儿了：

blocks.html_en:
Attorney Julie Le was representing the government at a hearing over ICE’s failure to follow court orders and immediately release people that it had wrongfully detained. When Judge Jerry Blackwell asked why the agency is not complying, Le said that the government was “overwhelmed” by the legal challenges to Operation Metro Surge in Minnesota, and that trying to get ICE to comply with court orders has required nonstop work for an office depleted by resignations. 

“I wish you would just hold me in contempt of court so I can get 24 hours of sleep,” Le said. “The system sucks, this job sucks, I am trying with every breath I have to get you what I need.”

sentences.en: Attorney Julie Le was representing the government at a hearing over ICE’s failure to follow court orders and immediately release people that it had wrongfully detained.
sentences.zh: 律师 Julie Le 代表政府出席听证会，指控 ICE 未能遵守法院命令并立即释放被错误拘留的人。

sentences.en: When Judge Jerry Blackwell asked why the agency is not complying, Le said that the government was “overwhelmed” by the legal challenges to Operation Metro Surge in Minnesota, and that trying to get ICE to comply with court orders has required nonstop work for an office depleted by resignations.
sentences.zh: 当杰里·布莱克威尔法官询问该机构为何不遵守时，勒表示，明尼苏达州“地铁激增”行动所面临的法律挑战让政府“不知所措”，而要让 ICE 遵守法院命令，就需要一个因辞职而精疲力竭的办公室不间断地工作。

sentences.en: “I wish you would just hold me in contempt of court so I can get 24 hours of sleep,” Le said.
sentences.zh: “我希望你能以藐视法庭罪判我，这样我就能睡 24 小时，”Le 说。

sentences.en: “The system sucks, this job sucks, I am trying with every breath I have to get you what I need.”
sentences.zh: “这个系统很糟糕，这份工作很糟糕，我每一次呼吸都在努力为你提供我需要的东西。”

blocks.html_en: Attorney Julie Le was representing the government at a hearing over ICE’s failure to follow court orders and immediately release people that it had wrongfully detained. When Judge Jerry Blackwell asked why the agency is not complying, Le said that the government was “overwhelmed” by the legal challenges to Operation Metro Surge in Minnesota, and that trying to get ICE to comply with court orders has required nonstop work for an office depleted by resignations.

sentences.en: Attorney Julie Le was representing the government at a hearing over ICE’s failure to follow court orders and immediately release people that it had wrongfully detained.
sentences.zh: 律师 Julie Le 代表政府出席听证会，指控 ICE 未能遵守法院命令并立即释放被错误拘留的人。

sentences.en: When Judge Jerry Blackwell asked why the agency is not complying, Le said that the government was “overwhelmed” by the legal challenges to Operation Metro Surge in Minnesota, and that trying to get ICE to comply with court orders has required nonstop work for an office depleted by resignations.
sentences.zh: 当杰里·布莱克威尔法官询问该机构为何不遵守时，勒表示，明尼苏达州“地铁激增”行动所面临的法律挑战让政府“不知所措”，而要让 ICE 遵守法院命令，就需要一个因辞职而精疲力竭的办公室不间断地工作。

blocks.html_en: “I wish you would just hold me in contempt of court so I can get 24 hours of sleep,” Le said. “The system sucks, this job sucks, I am trying with every breath I have to get you what I need.”

sentences.en: “I wish you would just hold me in contempt of court so I can get 24 hours of sleep,” Le said.
sentences.zh: “我希望你能以藐视法庭罪判我，这样我就能睡 24 小时，”Le 说。

sentences.en: “The system sucks, this job sucks, I am trying with every breath I have to get you what I need.”
sentences.zh: “这个系统很糟糕，这份工作很糟糕，我每一次呼吸都在努力为你提供我需要的东西。”

blocks.html_en: Given that kind of work environment, is it really any surprise that the few remaining DOJ lawyers would turn to hallucinating AI tools to “generate” their legal filings?

sentences.en: Given that kind of work environment, is it really any surprise that the few remaining DOJ lawyers would turn to hallucinating AI tools to “generate” their legal filings?
sentences.zh: 考虑到这种工作环境，剩下的少数司法部律师会求助于人工智能工具来“生成”他们的法律文件，这真的有什么奇怪的吗？

blocks.html_en: But if you’re starting to feel any ounce of sympathy for the DOJ here, let’s be clear: fuck that. If the DOJ can’t do their fucking job they shouldn’t be throwing people into jails, detainment centers, concentration camps, or anything of that nature. If they want to go around fighting habeas petitions, maybe don’t lock up so many people without any ounce of due process. And if they want to keep people detained then hire enough lawyers to handle the government’s case load.

sentences.en: But if you’re starting to feel any ounce of sympathy for the DOJ here, let’s be clear: fuck that.
sentences.zh: 但如果你开始对司法部产生一丝同情，我们就明确一点：去他妈的。

sentences.en: If the DOJ can’t do their fucking job they shouldn’t be throwing people into jails, detainment centers, concentration camps, or anything of that nature.
sentences.zh: 如果司法部不能完成他们该死的工作，他们就不应该把人扔进监狱、拘留中心、集中营或任何类似性质的地方。

sentences.en: If they want to go around fighting habeas petitions, maybe don’t lock up so many people without any ounce of due process.
sentences.zh: 如果他们想四处对抗人身保护令请愿书，也许就不要在没有任何正当程序的情况下关押这么多人。

sentences.en: And if they want to keep people detained then hire enough lawyers to handle the government’s case load.
sentences.zh: 如果他们想继续拘留人们，那就聘请足够的律师来处理政府的案件。

blocks.html_en: And if that’s too difficult because good lawyers have no interest in working for a fascist regime that is stomping all over basic fundamental freedoms in every direction, well, maybe work on that rather than locking innocent people up with no due process. Donald Trump’s administration put this DOJ in this position and there’s simply zero excuse for judges letting the DOJ get away with this sort of absolute bullshit.

sentences.en: And if that’s too difficult because good lawyers have no interest in working for a fascist regime that is stomping all over basic fundamental freedoms in every direction, well, maybe work on that rather than locking innocent people up with no due process.
sentences.zh: 如果这太困难了，因为优秀的律师没有兴趣为一个在各个方面践踏基本自由的法西斯政权工作，那么，也许可以努力做到这一点，而不是在没有正当程序的情况下将无辜者关起来。

sentences.en: Donald Trump’s administration put this DOJ in this position and there’s simply zero excuse for judges letting the DOJ get away with this sort of absolute bullshit.
sentences.zh: 唐纳德·特朗普政府将司法部置于这样的境地，法官们没有任何理由让司法部摆脱这种绝对的废话。

blocks.html_en: Judges should be issuing sanctions left and right. They should be reporting lawyers to ethics committees and the relevant bar associations. They should be demanding that the government actually obey the fucking law, and not deny anyone their rights.

sentences.en: Judges should be issuing sanctions left and right.
sentences.zh: 法官应该左右发出制裁。

sentences.en: They should be reporting lawyers to ethics committees and the relevant bar associations.
sentences.zh: 他们应该向道德委员会和相关律师协会报告律师。

sentences.en: They should be demanding that the government actually obey the fucking law, and not deny anyone their rights.
sentences.zh: 他们应该要求政府真正遵守该死的法律，而不是剥夺任何人的权利。

blocks.html_en: Want to stop the DOJ from locking people up with fictitious cases? Start issuing actual consequences to those lawyers and anyone else at the DOJ who had anything to do with this.

sentences.en: Want to stop the DOJ from locking people up with fictitious cases?
sentences.zh: 想要阻止司法部因虚构案件而关押人员吗？

sentences.en: Start issuing actual consequences to those lawyers and anyone else at the DOJ who had anything to do with this.
sentences.zh: 开始向那些律师和司法部任何与此事有关的其他人发出实际后果。

blocks.html_en: Filed Under: ai, ai hallucinations, doj, hala jarbou, ice detentions, izzeddin daghra, julie le

sentences.en: Filed Under: ai, ai hallucinations, doj, hala jarbou, ice detentions, izzeddin daghra, julie le
sentences.zh: 提起下： 艾, 艾幻觉, 司法部, 哈拉·贾布, 冰拘留, izzeddin daghra, 朱莉·勒

content_html_zh:
来自“这绝不能发生在自由社会”部门

你可能会认为，看到多名律师使用人工智能提交充满捏造引文的摘要而被当场抓获，会导致法律界的每个人——尤其是检察官——仔细检查自己的陈述。 当然，你错了。 最新的犯罪者不仅仅是某个随意的律师。 正是司法部本身引用了一个不存在的第六巡回法院案件，认为 ICE 被拘留者不应能够对阻止他缴纳已获准保释金的暂缓提出质疑，从而导致他被拘留。

这个故事涉及我们过去几年分别报道的两个不同的主题。 让它们发生碰撞会使两者变得更糟。

我们已经报道过很多案件，其中一方（或双方，有时是法官）的律师滥用人工智能为他们写作，生成虚构的案件来支持他们想要提出的任何论点。 这在很多层面上都令人不安，因为任何律师在向法院提交任何内容之前应该做的事情之一就是检查引文。 从历史上看，这是为了确保所引用的案例没有被推翻。 在这些情况下，这种情况不仅没有发生，而且他们实际上是在引用不存在的案例，引用完全虚构的先例。

我们在此涉及的其他故事是 ICE 和司法部如何践踏被拘留者的基本宪法权利。

这是被拘留者律师提交人身保护令的众多案件之一，也是这两条线索在实践中发生冲突的一个明显例子。 我会让哈拉·Y 法官来。 Jarbou 总结了所发生事件的基本情况：

申请人是一名被美国移民和海关执法局拘留的人，根据《美国法典》第 28 条的规定，通过提交人身保护令请愿书发起了这一诉讼。 § 2241. （宠物，ECF No.1。） 移民法官已向申请人提供 35,000 美元的保释金，但保释令被暂缓执行，等待根据 8 C.F.R. 向移民上诉委员会提出上诉。 § 1003.19(i) (2025)。 申请人辩称，第 1003.19(i) 条中的 90 天自动中止条款违反了第五修正案的正当程序条款，并寻求一项命令，要求政府允许他交纳保释金。 在该诉讼悬而未决期间，申请人的保证金令自动中止到期。 政府现在表示，保证金令重新生效，如果申请人缴纳 35,000 美元的保证金，他将被释放。 （状态报告，ECF 第 10 号。） 因此，法院认为人身保护令申请没有实际意义，并在不造成偏见的情况下予以驳回。

令人沮丧的是，被拘留者所质疑的 90 天“自动拘留”在实际案件得到判决之前就结束了，这使得整个事情变得毫无意义。

但… 法官还有别的事。 美国司法部在早些时候的一份文件中似乎有一个完全捏造的引文：

本案还有一个法院必须解决的问题。 政府在对法院最初的说明理由命令的回应中指出：最近，第六巡回法院重申，第 1226(e) 条禁止提出质疑“要求法院重新权衡保释决定所依据的证据或事后怀疑移民法官的自由裁量判决”。 参见泰勒诉。 霍特，724 F. App’x 387、392（第 6 环） 2018）（地方法院没有管辖权来审查 IJ 在申请人对潜逃风险判定提出质疑的情况下拒绝保释的情况）。 。 。 。 （政府的答复。 9、ECF 第 5 号。） 所引用的案例，泰勒诉。 Hott，未位于联邦附录的指定页面中。 事实上，第 387 页包含了不同的意见——Atkins v. CGI 技术。 & Sols., Inc.，724 F. App’x 383（第 6 环。 2018）——这是关于商业仲裁，而不是移民债券的决定。 在其研究中，法院无法识别标题为泰勒诉泰勒案的第六巡回法院案件。 霍特，或任何包含引用语言的联邦案件。 因此，这条引文似乎很可能是由生成人工智能（“AI”）产生的。

当两个私人当事人之间的民事案件中出现捏造的传票时，情况就已经够糟糕的了。 但这里我们实际上谈论的是涉及某人自由的案件。 政府正在立案人工智能生成的假案件？！？

我们应该感到愤怒。 但法官轻轻地打了他们一巴掌，并说：“请不要再这样做了”：

显然，任何使用人工智能的律师都必须仔细审查其工作产品，以确保引用的案例存在，并且引用准确、公平地代表了基本的判例法。 对本法庭的坦诚义务要求同样如此。 尽管法院目前不会对这种行为实施制裁，但毫无疑问，政府必须确保未来向法院提交的文件不包括不存在的判例法

而且，是的，法官在发布制裁时往往相当克制，在急于进行惩罚之前，常常给行为不端的律师更多的机会，而他们可能不应该得到这样的机会。 但话又说回来，这事关某人的自由。 司法部确实直接——无论是有意还是无意——以不存在的先例误导了法院。 那绝对不应该发生。

我们都知道司法部在聘请律师方面遇到了麻烦。 自唐纳德·特朗普重新上任以来，已有超过 10,000 名律师离开联邦政府。 他们为那些律师过去故意大幅减薪以写在简历上的工作提供巨额奖金。 几乎每个人都认为这是一场人员配置危机，以至于今年早些时候，一位显然疲惫不堪的美国助理检察官朱莉·勒 (Julie Le) 要求一位愤怒的法官认定她藐视法庭，这样她就可以睡一会儿了：

律师 Julie Le 代表政府出席听证会，指控 ICE 未能遵守法院命令并立即释放被错误拘留的人。 当杰里·布莱克威尔法官询问该机构为何不遵守时，勒表示，明尼苏达州“地铁激增”行动所面临的法律挑战让政府“不知所措”，而要让 ICE 遵守法院命令，就需要一个因辞职而精疲力竭的办公室不间断地工作。 “我希望你能以藐视法庭罪判我，这样我就能睡 24 小时，”Le 说。 “这个系统很糟糕，这份工作很糟糕，我每一次呼吸都在努力为你提供我需要的东西。”

考虑到这种工作环境，剩下的少数司法部律师会求助于人工智能工具来“生成”他们的法律文件，这真的有什么奇怪的吗？

但如果你开始对司法部产生一丝同情，我们就明确一点：去他妈的。 如果司法部不能完成他们该死的工作，他们就不应该把人扔进监狱、拘留中心、集中营或任何类似性质的地方。 如果他们想四处对抗人身保护令请愿书，也许就不要在没有任何正当程序的情况下关押这么多人。 如果他们想继续拘留人们，那就聘请足够的律师来处理政府的案件。

如果这太困难了，因为优秀的律师没有兴趣为一个在各个方面践踏基本自由的法西斯政权工作，那么，也许可以努力做到这一点，而不是在没有正当程序的情况下将无辜者关起来。 唐纳德·特朗普政府将司法部置于这样的境地，法官们没有任何理由让司法部摆脱这种绝对的废话。

法官应该左右发出制裁。 他们应该向道德委员会和相关律师协会报告律师。 他们应该要求政府真正遵守该死的法律，而不是剥夺任何人的权利。

想要阻止司法部因虚构案件而关押人员吗？ 开始向那些律师和司法部任何与此事有关的其他人发出实际后果。

提起下： 艾, 艾幻觉, 司法部, 哈拉·贾布, 冰拘留, izzeddin daghra, 朱莉·勒
